human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1449 scope, hospitals-and-health-systems admissions specificity, intake-access-and-initiation workflow coverage, healthcare-and-care-delivery domain research contract, authority-source coverage, scenario-to-ability mapping, PHI handling, clinical and payer boundaries, and specification-based runtime constraints.

review_notes: All required shared abilities, intake-access-and-initiation abilities, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are declared and mapped to passing scenarios.
Research coverage includes public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer or provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

The package stays specific to hospital admissions, ADT, pre-admit encounters, admission orders, bed placement, transfer packets, eligibility, referrals, authorizations, utilization review, privacy, source conflicts, and pre-execution admissions authority.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit around clinical admission decisions, medical necessity, level-of-care placement, isolation or safety review, payer authorization approval, privacy-release approval, coding, claims, denials, discharge planning, and care-transition ownership.

Authority sources are tied to inpatient admission order evidence, hospital medical record and utilization-review requirements, discharge or transfer boundaries, eligibility, prior authorization, PHI handling, encounter terminology, and patient-identification safety.

Scenario coverage includes normal admission readiness, missing prerequisites, active eligibility with missing referral or authorization, order ambiguity, patient identity and encounter conflicts, PHI controls, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, bed-placement bypass refusal, direct-admission transfer gaps, and adjacent-lane routing.
Deployment and commercialization artifacts explicitly keep the package at specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted.
notes: The package is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim retained-implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit implementation, connector validation, tenant retrieval validation, PHI control validation, and approval event.
